commands.review_summary:
The branch hardens Gateway MCP loopback schema projection and tool calls so unreadable or malformed tool metadata is omitted from `tools/list` and rejected by `tools/call` while healthy tools remain usable.

PR surface: Source +98, Tests +378. Total +476 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main directly reads tool metadata during schema projection and call lookup, and the PR tests encode those failure modes. I did not run tests because this review was read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual review is appropriate because this is a draft, protected maintainer-labeled PR and the remaining blocker is compatibility acceptance, not a narrow automated repair.

target.title: fix(gateway): harden MCP loopback tool schemas
target.body:
## Summary

- hardens Gateway MCP loopback tool listing against unreadable/malformed tool names, descriptions, and parameter schemas
- keeps healthy sibling tools listed/callable when malformed loopback tools are skipped
- makes `tools/call` use the same safely advertised schema surface so omitted tools are not executed through a malformed call path
- uses only synthetic `fuzzplugin` / `mockplugin` fixtures

## Verification

- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts --reporter=verbose`: 1 Gateway shard, 27 tests passed
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/gateway/mcp-http.handlers.ts src/gateway/mcp-http.schema.ts src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- touched-file scrub for the reported plugin/tool names, private paths, and private network literals: clean
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`: clean
- `OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`: AWS Crabbox run `run_b7107e0ae803`, lease `cbx_c4803b52b8ef`, type `c7a.8xlarge`, lanes `core`, `coreTests`, exit 0

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: malformed Gateway MCP loopback tool schemas no longer crash tool-list projection or leave a skipped/invalid tool callable through `tools/call`.

Real environment tested: fresh linked OpenClaw `gwt` worktree from `origin/main` `b7232db1b0e`, plus AWS Crabbox Linux remote changed gate.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: cherry-picked the narrow gateway slice onto fresh current main, ran the focused Gateway MCP test file, formatter check, diff check, scrub, autoreview, and AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed`.

Evidence after fix: tests prove unreadable loopback tool names/descriptions/parameter schemas are skipped without dropping healthy siblings, malformed union/property schemas do not crash listing, and calls to invalid or safely omitted tools are not executed.

Observed result after fix: Gateway MCP loopback clients get a safe tool list and healthy tools continue to work even when one loopback tool exposes bad schema metadata.

What was not tested: no full live MCP client round trip; behavior is covered by Gateway MCP loopback tests and the remote changed gate.
target.author: vincentkoc
